reddit_title: Spinal cord injury from a car crash in Montgomery, AL — what legal steps should the family take first?
reddit_body:
If someone in your family suffered a spinal cord injury in an Alabama car accident, the most important thing to know upfront is that Alabama has a strict 2-year statute of limitations on personal injury claims. That clock starts from the date of the crash, not the date of diagnosis or hospital discharge. Missing it almost always means losing your right to sue entirely.

Alabama also follows pure contributory negligence, which is one of the harshest standards in the country — if the injured person is found even 1% at fault, they can be barred from recovering anything. This makes early evidence preservation critical: accident scene photos, witness statements, police reports, black box data if a vehicle is involved, and medical records documenting the injury timeline all matter enormously.

For cases this serious, you really want an attorney who specifically handles catastrophic injury claims, not a general practice lawyer. Life-care planning, expert witnesses, and long-term damages calculations are all part of a well-built spinal cord injury case. If you're not sure where to start, wreckmatch.com offers a free matching service that connects crash victims with vetted attorneys who handle exactly these kinds of cases.
